Summary: Aayush Sinha is a B.Tech CSE with AIML student (Batch 2026) with a strong portfolio in GenAI, Machine Learning, and full-stack development. His experience includes internships at Prodigal AI and IBM, where he contributed to significant improvements in system efficiency and user experience. He has developed innovative projects such as a GenAI + Vision system for plant disease detection, a CNN-based model for autonomous vehicle traffic prediction, an AI shopping agent, and a RAG-based knowledge assistant, demonstrating expertise in deep learning, natural language processing, and scalable system design.
Key contributions: Built a GenAI knowledge-retrieval system using Gemini API, semantic search, and embeddings, cutting query time by 50%.
Automated business insights using a modular Telegram bot, reducing manual reporting by 80%.

Developed a full-stack GenAI + Vision system for plant disease detection (38 classes) with 98%+ accuracy on 70k+ images, integrating Gemini for diagnosis reports and an LLM-grounded reasoning layer for action-oriented recommendations.
Created a CNN-based machine learning model for autonomous vehicles, achieving 95% accuracy in traffic rule prediction from 40,000+ traffic board images and optimizing inference speed by 30%.
Currently building an AI Shopping Agent to autonomously complete checkout flows using LLM reasoning + API actions.

Developed a RAG-based Knowledge Assistant supporting 3+ document formats and handling 1,000+ document chunks, reducing query response time by 40% through efficient similarity search using LlamaIndex + ChromaDB.
Fixed 15+ UI bugs weekly and boosted overall customer satisfaction as a Frontend Engineering Intern at IBM.
Optimized API data handling, reducing page load times by ~30% and improving code maintainability at IBM.
Methodology:
The approach involves a combination of GenAI, deep learning, and full-stack development. Key methodologies include: 
- **GenAI & NLP:** Utilizing Gemini API, semantic search, embeddings, LLM reasoning, RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) with LlamaIndex and ChromaDB for vector storage and efficient similarity search.
- **Machine Learning & Deep Learning:** Employing CNNs (Convolutional Neural Networks) with frameworks like Tensorflow, Keras, Pytorch, and computer vision libraries like OpenCV.
- **Backend Development:** Using Flask, FastAPI, Uvicorn, Springboot, Java, PostgreSQL, and Firebase for scalable and real-time applications.
- **Frontend Development:** Building user interfaces with HTML, JS, CSS, React, Next.js, Streamlit, and CLI.
- **Agentic AI:** Designing systems with LLM reasoning combined with API actions for autonomous task completion.
- **Deployment & Tools:** Leveraging GitHub, JIRA, Postman, Vercel, and Render for version control, project management, and deployment.

Implementation ideas: **GenAI Knowledge Retrieval:** Build scalable backend modules for traceable, verifiable document-grounded AI answers using Gemini API, semantic search, and embeddings.

**AI Crop Doctor:** Integrate GenAI (Gemini) with computer vision (CNN) in a full-stack application (Flask, Firebase) to provide real-time plant disease diagnosis, detailed reports, and action-oriented recommendations via an LLM-grounded reasoning layer.
**Drive-Detect:** Deploy CNN-based models (PyTorch, OpenCV) via Flask/FastAPI for edge-device integration and real-time traffic sign recognition in autonomous driving scenarios.

**Aycart (AI Shopping Agent):** Develop a robust frontend (React/Next.js) and backend (Springboot/Java) architecture that supports AI-driven cart actions, enabling LLM reasoning to autonomously complete checkout flows and facilitate future smart features.

**Knowledge Assistant:** Implement a RAG system using LlamaIndex and ChromaDB to generate and store vector embeddings, providing efficient natural-language Q&A across diverse document formats through user-friendly interfaces (CLI/Streamlit).
**Business Automation:** Utilize modular Telegram bots to automate routine business tasks and generate insights, significantly reducing manual reporting efforts.
